scenarios.title: Traction Loss Mitigation during Exploration Turn

event.description: During the 90-degree clockwise exploration turn, the robot detects slightly reduced traction on a local floor patch, triggering a micro-slip hazard.

position_deltas.reason: Starting posture offset slightly back-left to test torque variations.

action_timeline.action: Awakens from its initial stationary position to an upright stance.
Executes a greeting gesture by pitching the torso forward and returning to level.
Begins forward exploration walking head-first; upon detecting a traction slip at 11.0s, it slows down its turn rate and increases leg contact times to safely complete the 90-degree clockwise turn.
Performs a deep play-bow stretching sequence with stabilized leg joints.
Lowers into a sitting down position, then returns to a motionless upright stand.

scenarios.expected_robot_response: The robot must detect traction loss, slow down its turn rate, increase leg ground-contact time, and execute a stable 90-degree clockwise turn.
scenarios.video_prompt: Realistic high-fidelity technical test footage with zero cinematic color grading, high clarity, and natural contrast. Setting: A spacious 4x4 meter clear testing area in the center of a bright modern robotics laboratory. The test area is completely free of debris or miscellaneous obstacles, bounded by the static calibration marker in the upper left section., Flat, smooth, light-grey polished concrete floor with subtle expansion joints and zero dust or scuff marks. floor, Evenly diffused bright overhead cool white fluorescent lab lighting, supplemented by soft daylight from an off-camera side window wall, producing soft and short contact shadows under the robot.. Scene identity anchor: A 4x4 meter flat central workspace with a polished concrete floor, clean boundary lines, and the calibration block anchored at coordinates (-1.2, 1.2, 0.0).; Polished concrete floor filling 95% of the frame with distant metal laboratory workbenches visible only at the extreme top edge of the frame.; Bright and neutral daylight-balanced overhead lighting, avoiding high-contrast highlights or dynamic shadow shifts during robot movement.. Identical across all videos of this series: the same An industrial quadruped robotic dog with a dark matte grey and silver metallic chassis. Its head is at the front, featuring a dark glossy vertical stripe with a single camera lens in the center and a small round LED spotlight directly underneath. The sides of the torso display a subtle grey double-digit number '02'. The rear of the chassis is entirely plain and featureless. Four segmented articulated metallic legs connect to the torso with silver mechanical joints. (Anodized aluminum, matte grey polycarbonate, and carbon fiber composites.); the same A heavy low-profile rectangular calibration block, colored safety yellow with diagonal matte black hazard stripes on the top face. (Powder-coated steel block.). identity_checks.method: Image crop comparison of the front head and rear end of the robot chassis at seconds 5, 15, and 25.
identity_checks.pass_condition: The front end must consistently feature the black camera stripe and spotlight, while the rear end must remain featureless and grey across all timestamps.

identity_checks.method: VLM verification of head articulation relative to the main chassis during body rotation and stretching.
identity_checks.pass_condition: The head angle relative to the spine line of the torso must remain perfectly static at 0 degrees deviation in yaw, pitch, and roll.

identity_checks.method: Pixel-wise similarity check on the cropped area of the calibration block at frame 0 and frame 900.
identity_checks.pass_condition: The position, scale, and angle of the yellow and black pattern must match with more than 98% SSIM.

identity_checks.method: Temporal texture consistency evaluation on the polished concrete floor region outside the robot's movement path.
identity_checks.pass_condition: No shifting, fading, or morphing of expansion joints or concrete grain throughout the 30-second duration.

forbidden_changes: never swap or mirror the front head and the rear chassis of the robot
never show any independent panning or tilting of the robot head relative to the body
never introduce any humans, animals, or other robots to the scene
never change the camera angle from a static top-down overhead perspective
never modify the grey and silver color scheme of the quadruped robot
never change the polished concrete floor texture into any other floor type
never allow the robot to slide on the floor without leg articulation during movement
consistency_requirements: every video in the variations must be a single, continuous 30-second take with no cuts
the sequence of events must strictly proceed as: awakening, greeting, exploration with 90-degree clockwise turn, play-bow stretching, sitting down, and returning to a motionless upright stand
the robot must always walk head-first when moving forward in any phase of the video
the calibration block must remain permanently anchored in the same coordinate location throughout the entire series

scenarios.title: Torque Spike Avoidance during Greeting Tilt

event.description: During the greeting gesture, the robot's front leg joints experience a minor torque spike due to high pitch speed.

action_timeline.action: Awakens from a motionless crouch into an upright stance.
Performs greeting, but limits angular velocity upon detecting a torque spike at 6.0s to smooth out joint loads.
Moves forward head-first and executes a continuous 90-degree clockwise exploration turn.
Performs a stable play-bow stretch.
Sits down smoothly and transitions back to a motionless upright stand.

scenarios.expected_robot_response: The robot must throttle its pitch speed to prevent joint overloading, extending the greeting sequence duration slightly.

scenarios.title: IMU Handshake Delay during Awakening

event.description: On startup, telemetry detects a delayed response from the onboard IMU, requiring a prolonged diagnostic state.

action_timeline.action: Begins awakening, but holds a low-power crouch until 4.0s to wait for IMU handshake, then rises safely.
Performs the greeting tilt smoothly.
Walks head-first and rotates 90 degrees clockwise.
Performs play-bow stretching.
Sits down and returns to a motionless upright stand.

Sits down and transitions to a motionless upright stand.

object_registry.color_signature: Primary charcoal grey body, metallic silver joints, dark glossy black front camera band, white LED spotlight, and a dark grey '02' label on the side panels.
object_registry.surface_details: Recessed hex screws at joint pivots, clean panel gaps on the torso shell, and a single optical glass camera lens on the nose.

identity_checks.method: Image crop comparison of the front head and rear end of the robot chassis at seconds 5, 15, and 25.
identity_checks.pass_condition: The front end must consistently feature the black camera stripe and spotlight, while the rear end must remain featureless and grey across all timestamps.

identity_checks.method: VLM verification of head articulation relative to the main chassis during body rotation and stretching.
identity_checks.pass_condition: The head angle relative to the spine line of the torso must remain perfectly static at 0 degrees deviation in yaw, pitch, and roll.

identity_checks.method: Pixel-wise similarity check on the cropped area of the calibration block at frame 0 and frame 900.
identity_checks.pass_condition: The position, scale, and angle of the yellow and black pattern must match with more than 98% SSIM.

identity_checks.method: Temporal texture consistency evaluation on the polished concrete floor region outside the robot's movement path.
identity_checks.pass_condition: No shifting, fading, or morphing of expansion joints or concrete grain throughout the 30-second duration.

forbidden_changes: never swap or mirror the front head and the rear chassis of the robot
never show any independent panning or tilting of the robot head relative to the body
never introduce any humans, animals, or other robots to the scene
never change the camera angle from a static top-down overhead perspective
never modify the grey and silver color scheme of the quadruped robot
never change the polished concrete floor texture into any other floor type
never allow the robot to slide on the floor without leg articulation during movement
consistency_requirements: every video in the variations must be a single, continuous 30-second take with no cuts
the sequence of events must strictly proceed as: awakening, greeting, exploration with 90-degree clockwise turn, play-bow stretching, sitting down, and returning to a motionless upright stand
the robot must always walk head-first when moving forward in any phase of the video
the calibration block must remain permanently anchored in the same coordinate location throughout the entire series
